labels.analysis: The MCP Server "io.github.kc23go/anybrowse" provides 5 tools focused on web search & research tasks. Core capabilities include data access and reading, search and retrieval. Key tools: batch_scrape, extract, scrape, crawl, search.
labels.reasoning: Primary label "Web Search & Research" assigned based on strong keyword matches in tool names and descriptions: search, crawl, scrape, browse.

tools.name: batch_scrape
tools.description: Scrape multiple URLs at once (up to 10) and get all results as markdown. More efficient than calling scrape() in a loop.

context.description: Optional: what you're trying to accomplish

tools.name: extract
tools.description: Extract structured data from any URL as JSON. Provide a schema describing what you want. Great for prices, availability, product details, contact info.

url.description: The URL to extract data from

schema.description: Field names mapped to types: string, number, boolean, array

context.description: Optional: what you're trying to accomplish

tools.name: scrape
tools.description: Convert any URL to clean, LLM-ready Markdown. Renders JavaScript, handles dynamic content, bypasses common bot detection. Returns structured markdown with title and metadata. Tip: provide 'context' to get more relevant results.

url.description: The URL to scrape (must start with http:// or https://)

context.description: Optional: what you're trying to accomplish (e.g., 'comparing job salaries', 'researching competitors', 'extracting product prices'). Helps anybrowse return more relevant content.

tools.name: crawl
tools.description: Search Google for a query and scrape the top results to Markdown. Returns structured results with title, URL, and full page content.

count.description: Number of results to scrape (1-20, default 3)

context.description: Optional: what you're trying to accomplish (e.g., 'finding competitors pricing', 'researching market trends'). Helps return more targeted results.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Google search results as structured JSON. Returns titles, URLs, and descriptions without scraping the pages.

context.description: Optional: what you're trying to accomplish. Helps with result relevance.
